Interface DatabaseSupport

All Known Implementing Classes:
AbstractDatabaseSupport, CockroachDBSupport, DefaultSupport, MySQLSupport, PostgresSupport
public interface DatabaseSupport
Database-specific support interface for data generation and SQL handling.

This interface defines the single contract for database-specific implementations: mapping a Column to an appropriate DataGenerator. The cross-database defaults and the per-JDBCType registry live in AbstractDatabaseSupport; database-specific subclasses customize generation by registering or replacing factories rather than implementing this interface directly.

Common implementations include PostgresSupport, MySQLSupport, CockroachDBSupport, and DefaultSupport for generic databases.

Author:
Tim Veil
See Also:
  • DataGenerator
  • Column
  • AbstractDatabaseSupport
  • GeneratorFactory

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    default String
    batchRewriteUrlParameter()
    Returns the JDBC-URL parameter name that enables this driver's batch rewrite optimization — collapsing a JDBC batch into a single multi-row INSERT — or null if the database has no such parameter.
    static DatabaseSupport
    forConnection(Connection connection)
    Selects a DatabaseSupport by reading the product name from the connection's metadata.
    static DatabaseSupport
    forProduct(String productName)
    Selects a DatabaseSupport for the given JDBC product name (as reported by DatabaseMetaData.getDatabaseProductName()), so callers don't have to hardcode a specific implementation.
    DataGenerator<?>
    getDataGenerator(Column column, RandomGenerator random)
    Creates an appropriate data generator for the given column based on its JDBCType (and, where relevant, its database-specific type name).
    default Map<String, ColumnConstraint>
    readConstraints(Connection connection, String schema, String table)
    Reads the value constraints (CHECK constraints and enum/domain allowed values) for a table's columns, so the fill engine can generate values that satisfy them (issue #479).

  • Method Details

    • getDataGenerator

      DataGenerator<?> getDataGenerator(Column column, RandomGenerator random)
      Creates an appropriate data generator for the given column based on its JDBCType (and, where relevant, its database-specific type name).
      Parameters:
      column - the column metadata including type and constraints
      random - the random number generator for seeded data generation
      Returns:
      a data generator appropriate for the column type
      Throws:
      UnsupportedOperationException - if the column type is not supported

    • batchRewriteUrlParameter

      default String batchRewriteUrlParameter()
      Returns the JDBC-URL parameter name that enables this driver's batch rewrite optimization — collapsing a JDBC batch into a single multi-row INSERT — or null if the database has no such parameter.

      Bloviate fills through a Connection/DataSource it does not own, so it cannot add this parameter to the URL after the fact. Instead, the fill engine reads it to warn when the parameter is absent (it is often the single biggest fill speedup), and JdbcUrls uses it to help callers build a correctly-parameterized URL.

      Known values: PostgreSQL reWriteBatchedInserts, MySQL rewriteBatchedStatements. CockroachDB ignores batch rewrite, so it returns null; the generic DefaultSupport also returns null.

      Returns:
      the batch-rewrite URL parameter name, or null if not applicable
      Since:
      2.10.0

    • readConstraints

      default Map<String, ColumnConstraint> readConstraints(Connection connection, String schema, String table)
      Reads the value constraints (CHECK constraints and enum/domain allowed values) for a table's columns, so the fill engine can generate values that satisfy them (issue #479). The default returns an empty map (no constraint awareness); database-specific implementations override it with vendor catalog queries.

      Constraint reading is best-effort and must never fail a fill: an implementation that cannot read the catalog should log and return what it has.

      Parameters:
      connection - an open connection to query the catalog with
      schema - the table's schema (may be null for the default schema)
      table - the table name
      Returns:
      constraints keyed by lower-cased column name; empty when none are found or supported
      Since:
      2.14.0

    • forProduct

      static DatabaseSupport forProduct(String productName)
      Selects a DatabaseSupport for the given JDBC product name (as reported by DatabaseMetaData.getDatabaseProductName()), so callers don't have to hardcode a specific implementation.

      Matching is case-insensitive and substring-based: names containing "cockroach" map to CockroachDBSupport, "mysql" to MySQLSupport, and "postgres" to PostgresSupport. Anything else (including null) falls back to DefaultSupport.

      CockroachDB note: CockroachDB is typically reached through the PostgreSQL JDBC driver, which reports its product name as "PostgreSQL", so such connections resolve to PostgresSupport. Because CockroachDBSupport extends PostgresSupport and adds no extra behavior, this resolves the same type handling (uuid, jsonb, inet, intervals, arrays, bit strings, ...); passing new CockroachDBSupport() explicitly is equivalent.

      Parameters:
      productName - the database product name, may be null
      Returns:
      the matching support, or DefaultSupport if none matches

    • forConnection

      static DatabaseSupport forConnection(Connection connection) throws SQLException
      Selects a DatabaseSupport by reading the product name from the connection's metadata. See forProduct(String) for the matching rules and the CockroachDB caveat.
      Parameters:
      connection - an open database connection
      Returns:
      the matching support, or DefaultSupport if none matches
      Throws:
      SQLException - if the connection metadata cannot be read
